steps.title: Find the shared Piketty claim
steps.content: Text 1 says Capital in the Twenty-First Century has a more rigorous structure than Capital and Ideology. Text 2 says Capital and Ideology has different aims than Piketty's earlier books. Both authors agree that the later book differs structurally from earlier work. The disagreement is about evaluation, not whether the structures differ.

steps.title: Reject one-sided claims
steps.content: Choice B is something Text 1 rejects because it calls the vegetarian-diet material a tedious digression. Choice C states only Text 1's negative judgment. Choice D overstates Text 2's Burton comparison as influence, and Text 1 never mentions Burton. The answer is A.
